title: Lastrea gymnopoda
taxonomy: Lastrea gymnopoda, also known as the Gymnopus fern, is a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae. This plant is native to tropical regions of Asia and is commonly found in shaded, humid environments. From a taxonomic perspective, Lastrea gymnopoda is classified as a member of the genus Lastrea, which is part of the larger division Pteridophyta. The genus Lastrea is further grouped within the family Dryopteridaceae, which is a part of the fern order Polypodiales. The classification of Lastrea gymnopoda reflects its evolutionary relationships and characteristics within the fern family.
uses: Lastrea gymnopoda has been found to have various medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant is traditionally used in herbal remedies to treat ailments such as fever, cough, and respiratory issues. Its extracts have also been studied for potential anticancer and antioxidant properties. In some cultures, the plant is used to treat skin conditions and wounds due to its antiseptic properties. However, further research is needed to fully understand the plant's medicinal uses and potential benefits.

preparations: Lastrea gymnopoda, also known as the Japanese tassel fern, has been used in traditional herbal medicine for various purposes. intro: Lastrea gymnopoda, commonly known as the Gymnopodium fern, is a species of fern in the family Blechnaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may aid in the treatment of various ailments such as fever, sore throats, and respiratory issues.
